steps.title: Connect the view and evidence
steps.content: The passage first gives the standard view among economists: stock market liberalization would make it easier for companies to raise money from investors. It then gives Chen's evidence: companies in 11 liberalizing countries received significant investment increases in the three years after liberalization.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice B misstates the timing because the increases occurred within the three years after liberalization, not only after three years had passed. Choice C invents serious negative effects that the passage never mentions. Choice D invents a comparison between Venezuela and Indonesia, but Chen's finding is an average across countries.

steps.title: Complete the inference
steps.content: The finding supports the economists' expectation because the observed investment increases line up with the prediction that liberalization would help companies raise funds. Empirical evidence matching scholarly consensus is the logical completion. The answer is A.
